question: [File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.consult / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who observes that the TORUS/TITAN_X pool has a very high secondsPerLiquidityCumulativesDelta (due to very low in-range liquidity during the observation window) cause harmonicMeanLiquidity to be computed as near-zero (uint128(secondsAgoX160 / (uint192(secondsPerLiquidityCumulativesDelta) << 32)) rounds to 0), violating the invariant that harmonicMeanLiquidity must accurately reflect the pool's liquidity, causing scoped impact: any downstream logic relying on harmonicMeanLiquidity for price bounds receives a near-zero value, potentially enabling extraction? Local proof idea: fuzz test with secondsPerLiquidityCumulativesDelta near type(uint160).max, assert harmonicMeanLiquidity is 0 and check if this affects any downstream logic.

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TORUS/TITAN_X pool price to a tick where TickMath.getSqrtRatioAtTick returns type(uint160).max cause getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 to compute ratioX192 = type(uint160).max * type(uint160).max which overflows uint256, violating the invariant that getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 must not overflow for any valid sqrtRatioX96 value, causing scoped impact: consult() or getTorusQuoteForTitanX reverts, DoS-ing all swap functions? Local proof idea: unit test with sqrtRatioX96 = type(uint160).max, assert no overflow in ratioX192 computation (note: type(uint160).max^2 > type(uint256).max).

[File: 'src/contracts/src/library/OracleLibrary.sol -> Scope: Critical fund extraction or protocol value drain by an unprivileged attacker'] [Function: OracleLibrary.getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 / TorusBuyAndProcess.getTorusQuoteForTitanX] Can an unprivileged attacker who manipulates the TORUS/TITAN_X pool price to a tick where sqrtRatioX96 is exactly type(uint128).max cause getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 to use the ratioX192 branch (sqrtRatioX96 <= type(uint128).max), computing ratioX192 = type(uint128).max * type(uint128).max = type(uint256).max - 2*type(uint128).max + 1 which fits in uint256, then Math.mulDiv(ratioX192, baseAmount, 1<<192) which could overflow for large baseAmount, violating the invariant that getQuoteForSqrtRatioX96 must not overflow for any valid inputs, causing scoped impact: getTorusQuoteForTitanX reverts, DoS-ing all swap functions?
